pi_items.source: HTTP HEAD request verified URL returns 200 OK, application/zip content type, 799734 bytes
Downloaded actual shapefile from URL, read ne_50m_admin_0_countries.dbf and analyzed with pyshp library
Analyzed downloaded shapefile using pyshp library - counted all points in all shapes
Standard value for Douglas-Peucker simplification at 50m resolution. At equator, 0.05 degrees ≈ 5.5 km, appropriate for web map simplification
Actual dataset contains 242 features; threshold of 200 allows for minor loss during simplification while ensuring output preserves most countries
Verifier checks for >= 30% reduction (verifier.py line 115); reasonable for web map optimization (targets ~70,000 vertices from 99,613)
Natural Earth website (line 145 of README.html) claims 258 countries, but actual shapefile v5.1.1 contains only 242 features
Task description requires export in EPSG:4326; Natural Earth data is in WGS84; prj file confirms GEOGCS[WGS 84]

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Natural Earth 50m Admin 0 Countries shapefile (v5.1.1) from the real, public data source at https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/50m/cultural/ne_50m_admin_0_countries.zip. The dataset contains 242 country features with 99,613 total vertices. The task applies Douglas-Peucker simplification with 0.05 degree tolerance to reduce geometry complexity for web use. The verifier expects output to preserve ≥200 features (achievable with 242 input) and achieve ≥30% vertex reduction (from 99,613 to ~70,000 target). Output must be valid GeoJSON in EPSG:4326 CRS.
